steps.title: Identify the roots of the polynomial
steps.content:
The equation $(x - 16)(x - 12)(x + 5)(x + 13) = 0$ is already factored. For a product to equal zero, at least one of its factors must be zero.
We can identify the roots by setting each factor equal to zero.

steps.title: Solve for x when each factor equals zero
steps.content:
• Setting \(x - 16 = 0\) yields \(x = 16\).
  • Setting \(x - 12 = 0\) yields \(x = 12\).
  • Setting \(x + 5 = 0\) yields \(x = -5\).
  • Setting \(x + 13 = 0\) yields \(x = -13\).
The positive solutions are therefore x = 16 and x = 12. Since we need the largest one, the answer is related to these two.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
